claim_text: After PR #14420 (pytest 9.0.x backport), `pytest.raises(..., match=...)` suppresses the original exception context when the raised exception type matches but the regex match fails. The implementation raises `AssertionError(self._fail_reason) from None`, so short traceback output reports the regex mismatch without surfacing `ValueError: actual` or chained-exception boilerplate.
title: [PR #14391/cd7592c4 backport][9.0.x] Use direct cause for raises match failures

evidence_items.summary: PR pytest-dev/pytest#14420 changes src/_pytest/raises.py:RaisesExc.__exit__ so match-failure AssertionError is raised `from None`, suppressing chained context from the originally raised exception.

New test testing/python/raises.py::TestRaises::test_raises_match_failure_suppresses_exception_context creates a ValueError('actual') under pytest.raises(..., match='expected'), runs pytest with --tb=short, asserts a regex mismatch AssertionError, and asserts the original ValueError plus chained-context messages are absent.

candidate_reason: automated initial selection: 3 files changed, 15 lines, merge_status=merged; manually reviewed and pinned as Phase 6.1'a worked example (small diff, single parameterized test, clear bugfix narrative)
claim_text: After PR #14407 (backport of #14382 to the 9.0.x branch), pytest's CLI accepts the `-V` short-flag as equivalent to `--version`: both produce the version string `pytest <X.Y.Z>` via the early fast-path in `_pytest/config/__init__.py:main()`, without loading the rest of the pytest infrastructure. Prior to this change `-V` was not in the early-version fast-path counter, so it would either error or trigger full plugin loading depending on the calling context. Provenance note: this PR is a 9.0.x backport — the original fix was authored by a community contributor in PR #14382 against main, then cherry-picked by maintainers; the diff is materially identical (changelog entry + src counter update + test parametrization).

evidence_items.summary: GitHub PR pytest-dev/pytest#14407 cherry-picks PR #14382 (community fix) onto branch 9.0.x. Diff: 3 files (changelog/14381.bugfix.rst, src/_pytest/config/__init__.py, testing/test_helpconfig.py); 15 changed lines. Production change: counter for the early fast-path in main() now sums --version + -V occurrences instead of only --version.

Test testing/test_helpconfig.py::test_version_less_verbose is parametrized over flag in ["--version", "-V"]. Both runs assert: result.ret == ExitCode.OK and result.stdout.str().strip() == f"pytest {pytest.__version__}". A clean pytest run on this scope at head_sha 480809ae demonstrates -V no longer loads plugins.

title: [PR #14382/d72943a5 backport][9.0.x] Fix `-V` to show version information

claim_text: After PR #1529 (attrs), `attr.fields()` accepts an attrs INSTANCE in addition to an attrs CLASS. When called on an instance whose `type(...)` carries `__attrs_attrs__`, the implementation in `src/attr/_make.py:fields()` recurses with `type(cls)` and returns the same tuple `fields(C)` would return. Non-attrs instances (e.g. `object()`) still raise `TypeError` with the updated message 'Passed object must be a class or attrs instance.' This is a new capability previously unsupported in the public API.

evidence_items.summary: PR python-attrs/attrs#1529 modifies src/attr/_make.py:fields(). Production change: when the input is not a class AND not a generic_base, the function now checks `getattr(type(cls), '__attrs_attrs__', None)` and recurses with type(cls) if the instance is from an attrs class. The TypeError message is updated to 'Passed object must be a class or attrs instance.'.

tests/test_make.py::TestFields::test_instance is REWRITTEN: was raising TypeError on `fields(C())`; now asserts `fields(C()) is fields(C)` (parametrized via @given(simple_classes()) from hypothesis). A new sibling test test_handler_non_attrs_instance asserts TypeError still raised for non-attrs instances like `object()`.

title: Add instance support to attrs.fields()

claim_text: After PR #690 (sqlite-utils), the convert helper in sqlite_utils/utils.py:_compile_code accepts callable references as code (e.g. `r.parsedate` or `json.loads`) in addition to the existing expression-and-function-body forms. The new path tries `eval(code, globals)` and returns the result if it is callable; falls back to the previous compilation path on failure. Imports are now applied BEFORE all approaches so the callable-reference path can resolve names like `json.loads` from `--import json`. New regression tests parametrize over four code forms: `r.parsedate`, `recipes.parsedate`, `r.parsedate(value)`, `recipes.parsedate(value)`.

evidence_items.summary: PR sqlite-utils#690 modifies sqlite_utils/utils.py:_compile_code: imports applied first; new try/eval path for direct callable references; fall-through to existing function-body compilation. 13 added / 2 removed lines in the production file.

Two new tests in tests/test_cli_convert.py: test_convert_callable_reference parametrized over four code forms (callable refs vs calls); test_convert_callable_reference_with_import exercises `json.loads` with --import json.

title: Allow callable references in sqlite-utils convert, closes #686

candidate_reason: automated initial selection: 9 files changed, 98 lines, merge_status=merged; manually reviewed and pinned as Phase 6.1'c HOLDOUT case (clear repair_needed semantics, has explicit regression test, non-trivial code change, NOT a backport, NOT release-prep)
claim_text: After PR #680 (sqlite-utils), the column_type_mapping in db.py maps Python `float`, `decimal.Decimal`, and the numpy float types (np.float16/32/64) to SQLite `REAL` instead of `FLOAT`. Per SQLite documentation, `REAL` is the canonical SQLite type affinity for floating-point values; `FLOAT` is a non-canonical alias accepted by the parser but not used by the storage class system. The PR also extends `VALID_COLUMN_TYPES` and the cli choice list to accept both `REAL` and `FLOAT` for backward compatibility, so callers specifying `--type float` or `--type FLOAT` continue to work, but the produced schema is `REAL`. New regression test `test_csv_detect_types_creates_real_columns` asserts that a CSV with float values + --detect-types produces a schema with `weight REAL`.

evidence_items.summary: PR sqlite-utils#680 modifies sqlite_utils/db.py COLUMN_TYPE_MAPPING: float and decimal.Decimal map to REAL (was FLOAT). numpy types (np.float16/32/64) also map to REAL. cli.py VALID_COLUMN_TYPES extends to (INTEGER, TEXT, FLOAT, REAL, BLOB) for backward compatibility.

New test test_csv_detect_types_creates_real_columns in tests/test_cli.py: imports a CSV with float column, runs --detect-types, asserts the produced schema string contains `"weight" REAL`. Six existing tests in test_create.py and test_cli.py updated to expect REAL instead of FLOAT.

title: Use REAL not FLOAT as SQLite column type

Phase 6.1' corpus-quality maintenance v1 (ADR-65, 2026-04-29): DEMOTED to train. Reason: Phase 6.1'h freeze-rule pass produced status=diagnostic_only with pytest rc=1 due to over-broad operator-authored test_scope (class-level TestCallsiteParameterAdder included pre-existing tests unrelated to PR #761 that fail in the isolated venv); the two specific tests added by PR #761 (test_qual_name_structlog, test_qual_name_logging_origin_absent) each pass individually. Per cgpro QA/A47 corpus-quality maintenance protocol: demote-and-replace, not rewrite Phase 6.1'h result.
claim_text: After PR #761 (structlog), CallsiteParameterAdder accepts a new CallsiteParameter.QUAL_NAME enum value that emits the qualified name (`co_qualname`) of the callsite — class-scope-aware (e.g. `MyClass.my_method`) — instead of just the function name. Requires Python 3.11+ because `frame.f_code.co_qualname` was added in 3.11. The QUAL_NAME parameter is deliberately excluded from the LogRecord-derived path: stdlib LogRecord does not have qual_name, so when the event originates from stdlib logging the QUAL_NAME field is absent (negative-path test). Provides a new capability for structured observability.

evidence_items.summary: PR structlog#761 adds CallsiteParameter.QUAL_NAME enum value + _get_callsite_qual_name handler in src/structlog/processors.py. Handler reads frame.f_code.co_qualname (Python 3.11+ only). CHANGELOG.md notes the addition under 25.5.0.

Two new tests in tests/processors/test_processors.py: test_qual_name_structlog (asserts qual_name endswith ClassName.method, skipif Python <3.11) and test_qual_name_logging_origin_absent (asserts QUAL_NAME absent when event originates from stdlib LogRecord).

title: Add CallsiteParameter.QUAL_NAME
